lesson: Aquarium debug UI uses a code-first Direct2D/DirectWrite overlay with explicit bound controls. Rect math must distinguish edges from width/height, controls need base/hover/active feedback, tooltips live in declarations, and long mode selectors should be named option rows instead of numeric sliders.
Renderer presentation knobs belong in a typed CultCache graphics settings document, not process folklore. Current durable knobs are render debug mode, exposure, bloom intensity, and bloom veil.
Use the MathWorld teardrop curve for the cursor profile with m=6, revolved into an SDF locator. Keep ripples as geometry, material as brass GGX metal, and avoid ambient fill.

Runtime volumetrics, cloud/fog research, and the D3D11 scene renderer are shelved because they distract from Aquarium's Epiphany-client mission. Git history holds the experiment; live repo state should not preserve compatibility husks or dead guidance for removed medium settings.

Medium/froxel render targets, structured buffers, shader bindings, scene outputs, post history lanes, and medium debug paths were removed from the live D3D12 renderer. Future atmospheric or field transport work needs a new explicit pass contract rather than reviving shelved scaffolding.
Blue-noise assets are retained for future dithering. Self and future other Selves emit through an explicit body light buffer; generic field instances are not the lighting authority.

Studio HDR reflection lighting is baked as a PMREM DDS cubemap: roughness selects higher mips, and the bake samples the source latlong by output direction so cubemap faces are generated from one continuous domain instead of filtered as isolated square images.

The Studio3 PMREM asset is live in the scene as a D3D12 TextureCube bound at t22. The renderer owns a narrow RGBA16F DX10 DDS cubemap loader, keeps upload resources alive until the copy fence completes, and the scene shader uses roughness-scaled SampleLevel for specular material reflections only.

Grid linework was replaced with a terminating perfect mirror surface. Grid hits now write the primary scene packet with FIELD_ID_GRID, terrain normal, full coverage, and Studio3 PMREM mip 0 reflection instead of using the transparent event lane.

Aquarium world space is Z-up while the baked Studio3 PMREM cubemap is sampled as Y-up. Every PMREM lookup must convert world directions with (x, z, y) before TextureCube sampling, or the HDRI appears sideways.

Active reflection probe switched to Studio2 from E:\Projects\Aetheria-Economy\Assets\Textures\studio2.hdr. The renderer loads Assets/Textures/studio2_pmrem.dds through the existing D3D12 TextureCube PMREM path, and the unused Studio3 DDS was cut from live assets.

Self's Grid gravity bowl now uses a 17-world-unit radius and an Aetheria-style radial cosine wave. The brush equation is mirrored across D3D12Grid, CPU body-light placement, D3D12Scene body centers, and D3D12Post temporal reprojection; body centers sit at Grid height plus 2x radius.
Grid reflection roughness is now slope-driven: flat regions sample higher Studio2 PMREM mips to avoid an over-literal HDRI mirror, while sloped or waved regions sharpen toward mip 0.

Flat Grid reflection now tops out at Studio2 PMREM mip 3, and ray misses use Studio2 PMREM mip 3 as the soft background so the reflective Grid is not contrasted against black void. Low-resolution mip texels are hidden with a small multi-direction cone average rather than one magnified TextureCube sample.
Studio2 PMREM was rebaked at 512px per cubemap face with 10 mips, so mip 3 is 64px per face instead of 32px. Shader roughness LOD max moved to 9 to match the chain.

Diffuse IBL is not GGX PMREM with a softer mip. Studio2 diffuse lighting now uses a separate irradiance cubemap baked by cosine-weighted hemisphere convolution, stores the Li*cos(theta) irradiance integral, and shades non-metal bodies with Fresnel-gated albedo/PI diffuse response.

Rider may open Aquarium.Engine.slnx with empty project trees even though dotnet can build it. Keep Aquarium.Engine.sln as the Rider-facing classic solution while Aquarium.Engine.slnx remains the compact CLI solution.

Epiphany agent bodies should be role-specific SDF organs driven by published CultNet surfaces: identity from epiphany.agent_memory, motion from epiphany.agent_heartbeat, posture from surface roles/coordinator/role_result, Face output from surface.face, and continuity pressure from pressure/reorient surfaces. Body and Imagination are the first implementation pair because they exercise grounded graph mass and generative harmonic bloom against the same AgentVisualState contract.

Agent orbit lanes are home anchors, not cages. Role bodies may wander a bounded distance from their designated orbit slots for expression, and vertical animation should come from CPU-visible gravity-well pulses so Grid height, body centers, shadows, and temporal reprojection share the same placement truth.

The Epiphany agent visual-language doc must specify spatial derivation, not describe role bodies with directionless vibes. Agent SDF orientation now has a world coordinate contract, fixed home orbit slots, named anchors, fallback chains, normalized state scalars, and concrete workDirection derivation for Hands.

Do not push Aquarium spatial authority onto Epiphany. Epiphany publishes semantic surfaces; Aquarium owns world placement. Agent visual anchors such as evidenceAnchor, artifactAnchor, workAnchor, reviewAnchor, and continuityAnchor are deterministic Aquarium-local projections from semantic state, not CultNet world coordinates emitted by Epiphany.

The Epiphany agent SDF visual spec should avoid presentation uncertainty. Remove slash-pairs and standalone 'or' alternatives from the visual-language document; choose one mathematical form per organ, favor sphere intersections, torus arcs, trefoil and lemniscate tubes, superquadrics, geodesic ribs, logarithmic spirals, rounded octahedra, and cardioid seeds over generic ellipsoid/capsule placeholders.

Agent role SDFs should render as bounded per-agent implicit objects, not one scene-global mega-SDF. Use conservative sphere-tracing distances, proxy rasterization, one role SDF selected after proxy hit, local bounds for expensive features, post-hit normals, cheap shadows, pixel-footprint hit epsilon, LOD tiers, and debug views for step count, proxy hits, material id, LOD, and SDF cost.
source: Hart sphere tracing, Enhanced Sphere Tracing, Inigo Quilez SDF bounding, CTSS, NVIDIA SDF grids

lesson: The fixed orbit bodies are now backed by an AgentVisualGpu buffer carrying current center, previous center, role id, state scalars, and LOD. D3D12Scene uses the buffer for bounded agent SDF hits; D3D12Post uses the same buffer for agent temporal reprojection. Body and Imagination are the first LOD 1 role SDFs, with debug modes for role, material, step count, LOD, and cost.

Do not animate role SDF lobes with raw atan2 angle fields inside the traced distance function; the polar branch cut creates visible cracks and sliced surfaces. Use continuous harmonic polynomial lobes over normalized directions, widen conservative proxy bounds, and reduce march step scale for distance-ish composite organs.

Continuous polar harmonics can still behave like a decorative pseudo-distance and leave visible petal gaps. Imagination now uses a central superquadric plus smooth-unioned ellipsoid petals, keeping the bloom continuous enough for bounded sphere tracing while preserving the five-lobed visual character.

Visible missing structure in Imagination came from shallow petal overlap plus a too-brave march through chained smooth unions. Petals now sit deeper into the core, use broader axes and larger union radii, and agent tracing uses 72 steps with smaller hit epsilon and 0.12 step scale.

Overcorrecting Imagination into a continuous blob destroyed the intended petal read. The better LOD 1 shape uses a small central seed, five narrow ellipsoid petals, and intentional negative space with only a tiny smooth union at the seed.

The fullscreen scene pass should own only background and Grid. Self, cursor, and role bodies now render through AgentVisualGpu-driven bounded proxy draws: one instanced conservative screen rectangle per object, object-local raymarching, and travel-derived depth writes so Grid and body occlusion stay honest.

D3D12Scene is now environment/Grid only. Bounded body proxy draws compile from D3D12Bodies.hlsl, reusable SDF math lives in D3D12SdfMath.hlsli, current agent character organs live in D3D12AgentCharacters.hlsli, and D3D12Renderer expands local includes before D3DCompile because the Vortice convenience compile path does not provide a usable include handler here. The cursor is now a luminous three-lobed hibiscus anchored on XY with its contact tip at Grid z=0.

Body proxy rendering now uses one shader file and one proxy pipeline per visible body slot: Face, Imagination, Eyes, Body, Hands, Self, and Cursor. Shared proxy plumbing lives in D3D12BodyCommon.hlsli and D3D12BodyProxy.hlsli, but body identity SDF and material code should stay split by body rather than collapsing into a single role or Self bucket.

The visible Epiphany agent body table is Self, Face, Imagination, Eyes, Body, Hands, Soul, and Life. Cursor remains a separate non-agent body. Renderer constants, body shader paths, proxy pipelines, and post reprojection counts should track seven role agents plus Self plus cursor, not the older five-role provisional slice.

Per-body proxy shaders must not rely on DrawInstanced StartInstanceLocation reaching SV_InstanceID for body identity. Each body shader now declares a shader-local BODY_INDEX constant, and shared proxy VS/PS use that constant for AgentVisualGpu lookup, proxy rectangle placement, and raymarch selection. This keeps per-body shader files transform-locked to their own visual row.

AgentVisualGpu row order is canonical and must match shader BODY_INDEX constants, body pipeline order, Grid brush indexing, and temporal reprojection: 0 Self, 1 Face, 2 Imagination, 3 Eyes, 4 Body, 5 Hands, 6 Soul, 7 Life, 8 Cursor. Roles-first buffer ordering breaks transforms even if each shader has its own file.

Body proxy quads and body proxy ray construction must use the same screen-space Y convention as the fullscreen scene pass. Clip-space proxy center should use projected.y directly, and the proxy pixel shader must convert SV_Position top-left pixel coordinates to the bottom-left pixel coordinates expected by rayDirectionForPixel. If they disagree, bodies appear to swim in camera space instead of staying grounded in world space.

The cursor hibiscus needs recognizable flower anatomy, not just three glossy petals. The cursor SDF now includes curled ruffled petals, a throat, a protruding staminal column with pollen beads, and a green calyx/contact stem, with a larger proxy bound to prevent clipping the expanded silhouette.

All role bodies are expected to carry thin visible structure, so conservative body SDF tracing is the shared default rather than a cursor-specific exception. Body proxy tracing now uses tighter hit epsilon, more steps, and smaller step scale through shared macros; faster tracing should only reappear through explicit LOD policy after it proves it preserves filaments, ribs, rings, petals, and other narrow organs.

Do not represent petals and other thin organs as fat ellipsoids with decorative displacement. The recursive lotus reference preserves delicate structure by using slab/intersection fields and scaling the distance estimate after its log-spherical mapping; Aquarium cursor petals now use thin clipped sheet SDFs, and the shared body marcher caps step length plus refines positive-to-negative sign crossings instead of trusting pseudo-distance composites blindly.

The cursor stamen and angled petals disappeared because the body proxy marcher accepted abs(distance) < epsilon as a hit on a pseudo-distance composite. Rays that passed near a petal sheet could terminate before reaching the actual stamen or petal intersection. The proxy marcher now requires distance <= 0 entry, refines positive-to-negative brackets, and uses a larger step budget to survive capped steps across the proxy volume.

The cursor hibiscus shape should be built from organ-appropriate SDF primitives instead of ellipsoid paste. Petals now use upward-curving tapered sheets with lens footprints and tapered thickness, the staminal column uses a curved tapered tube, and the green contact anatomy uses a connected tapered stem plus calyx bulb so the stem visibly reaches the flower.

The closer cursor objective is the pale pink five-petal hibiscus reference: broad translucent cupped petals, a folded central throat, a curved staminal column with side pollen filaments, and a green tapered calyx/stem ending at the XY contact point. The SDF now uses five tapered sheet petals, a simplified three-segment quadratic tube helper to keep shader compile time sane, side filament tubes, pollen bead spheres, and a curved tapered calyx core with sepals.

The hibiscus cursor petal should not be assembled from piecewise vertical panels and horizontal blades. Model each petal from one longitudinal coordinate: radial displacement grows quadratically so the root starts vertical, height follows a smooth cubic center curve, width and thickness grow from thin root to broad distal blade, and decorative ruffles stay out until the core SDF is stable and cheap.

When the hibiscus cursor starts turning into SDF Jenga, reset the function around anatomical parameters instead of stapling on fixes. The current reset keeps five petals as one cheap graph-style ribbon formula each: u comes from height, radial displacement is quadratic so roots start vertical, width and thickness grow with u, and decorative trig/filament swarms are cut until the core form is stable and fast.

Carry the cursor hibiscus lesson into agent bodies: start every role SDF from a coherent anatomical/form model with a small set of parameters that explain the silhouette across views. Prefer one governing coordinate, profile, width, thickness, symmetry, and material-region model over stitched primitives. Add ornament only after the core form is readable, fast, and stable; fine veins, glitter, filaments, and state pulses should begin as shading or LOD-gated overlays, not traced geometry.
